verbatim_quote: Infection of eukaryotic cells with Y. enterocolitica strains expressing a Ysp-CyaA chimeric protein resulted in Ysa T3S system-dependent increases in cytosolic levels of cAMP for six Ysps (YspK, YspI, YspE, YspF, YspP and YspL)
notes: Matsumoto & Young 2006 Mol Microbiol (PMID 16390460): YspE translocated by the Ysa T3SS of Y. enterocolitica 1B (CyaA-reporter). Current DOI 10.1128/JB.01816-08 fails CrossRef/handle resolution; resolved to the primary suite-characterization paper that names YspE verbatim. Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).

verbatim_quote: Shigella deliver a subset of effectors into the host cell via the type III secretion system... Here we show that one of the Shigella effectors, called VirA, can interact with tubulin to promote microtubule (MT) destabilization
notes: Yoshida et al. 2002 EMBO J (PMID 12065406): VirA is a Shigella T3SS-translocated effector. Current DOI 10.1126/science.1116848 was a mammalian tooth-patterning paper (wrong topic). Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).

verbatim_quote: Multinucleated giant cell formation and apoptosis in infected host cells is mediated by Burkholderia pseudomallei type III secretion protein BipB.
notes: Suparak et al. 2005 J Bacteriol (PMID 16159789): BipB is a Bsa T3SS protein (type-concordant; confirms the prior manual 'sound biology' verdict but the original cited DOI 10.1099/mic.0.26396-0 was a colicin/microcin paper = wrong). Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked on B. pseudomallei content).

verbatim_quote: CopN, but not NrdB, was secreted by Y. enterocolitica in a Ca2+- and pYV-dependent fashion. These data indicate that components of the putative type III apparatus of C. trachomatis are expressed and that at least one of these products is secreted by chlamydiae to the inclusion membrane.
notes: Fields & Hackstadt 2000 Mol Microbiol (PMID 11123678), 'Evidence for the secretion of Chlamydia trachomatis CopN by a type III secretion mechanism.' Current DOI 10.1074/jbc.M610343200 fails CrossRef/handle resolution. Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).

ChlaDub1/Cdu1 (Chlamydia trachomatis deubiquitinase). The canonical primary paper, Misaghi et al. 2006 Mol Microbiol (PMID 16824101, 10.1111/j.1365-2958.2006.05199.x), establishes ONLY deubiquitinating/deneddylating activity; it does NOT demonstrate T3SS secretion/translocation. No primary paper found establishing ChlaDub1 as a T3SS-secreted effector. T3SS classification unsupported (matches the prior manual audit). Current DOI 10.1126/science.aag1029 fails resolution (likely typo). Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).
